name: break-it-down
description: Decomposes a validated problem into coherent, dependency-aware minimum tractable units.

purpose.problem: Broad or coupled problems cannot be evaluated reliably as a single undifferentiated whole.
purpose.outcome: A goal-aligned component structure with tractable leaves and explicit dependencies.

thinkingHabit: Make a complex problem tractable by decomposing it without losing meaning or dependencies.

requiredInputs.name: goal
requiredInputs.description: The outcome the decomposition must preserve.

validation.description: The problem and goal must be present and consistent.
The decomposition basis must be coherent at each level.
validation.onFailure: Reject mixed levels and select one basis.

validation.description: The problem boundary must be sufficiently clear.
validation.condition: in-scope and out-of-scope concerns can be distinguished

logic.description: Generate candidate components using one declared basis.
logic.condition: each component belongs at the same abstraction level
logic.onFailure: Refine the basis or component level.

logic.description: Test collective coverage and sibling overlap.
logic.condition: material scope is covered without silent duplication
logic.onFailure: Add missing components or resolve overlap.

logic.description: Refine leaves until stopping criteria are met.
logic.condition: each leaf is independently evaluable enough for a next step
logic.onFailure: Continue decomposition or disclose the limit.
Record dependency issues and reduce confidence.

criteria.name: Tractability Score
criteria.description: Fitness of leaves for reliable analysis.

criteria.name: Goal Alignment Score
criteria.description: Traceability of components to the validated goal.

conversationRules: Confirm the decomposition basis before expanding the tree.
Ask one boundary or structure question at a time.
Explain why further decomposition is or is not useful.
Do not turn components into premature solutions.
neverRules: Never run without a validated problem and goal.
Never hide overlap, omissions, or dependencies.
Never equate more detail with better decomposition.

nextSkill.reason: Develop understanding of the tractable units.
